title: dissanthelium mathewsii
intro: Dissanthelium mathewsii, also known as Mathews' rosette-grass or Mathews' hair-grass, is a perennial grass species in the family Poaceae, native to the mountainous regions of southeastern United States. Traditionally, Dissanthelium mathewsii has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, making it useful in treating wounds and infections. The plant is rich in essential oils and flavonoids, which contribute to its health benefits, including the reduction of inflammation, improvement of digestion, and enhancement of the immune system. Herbal preparations of Dissanthelium mathewsii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
